commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4e517f1a31825cee9dfd8d5e849fe59995e71021)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an authDir-backed WhatsApp inbound queue, wires it into monitor acceptance/replay/finalization, delays read receipts, and updates monitor behavior test support.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main marks WhatsApp messages read before handler completion, and the PR race follows from re-entrant withFileLock plus fire-and-forget debouncer finalization. I did not run tests because this review was explicitly read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs author or maintainer revision plus refreshed real behavior proof on latest head; do not route to repair automation while the external proof gate is insufficient.

target.title: [Fix] Persist WhatsApp inbound delivery
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp inbound handling could acknowledge or dedupe a message before the handler finished, leaving no durable pending record if the gateway crashed mid-processing.
- Solution: Add an authDir-local durable inbound queue that accepts trackable messages before dispatch, completes them after successful/non-retryable finalization, and preserves retryable failures for replay.
- What changed: WhatsApp inbound processing now serializes acceptance/replay, stores pending Baileys messages with BufferJSON handling, delays read receipts until finalization, and drains queued work before socket close.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No WhatsApp auth, outbound delivery, provider, or public plugin API changes.

## Motivation

- This fixes a P1 message-loss failure where a gateway crash after inbound receipt but before handler completion could drop a WhatsApp message with no retryable state.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #50563
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: WhatsApp inbound records are written to the local durable queue before processing, stay pending across retry handoff, are removed only after completion, and completed IDs reject redelivery.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.4.1 local OpenClaw checkout with Node v24.14.0, production `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/durable-queue.ts`, and a real temporary authDir on disk.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `AUTH_DIR=$(mktemp -d) node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '<script imports durable-queue.ts, accepts one WhatsApp-shaped message, records retry, completes it, and accepts the same message again>'`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Terminal capture from `node --import tsx --input-type=module`:

```json
{
  "accepted": "accepted",
  "pendingAfterAccept": 1,
  "pendingAfterRetry": 1,
  "pendingAfterComplete": 0,
  "duplicateAfterComplete": "duplicate",
  "storeExists": true,
  "storeFile": "inbound-queue.json"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real authDir store created `inbound-queue.json`, retained one pending inbound record through retry recording, removed it on completion, and classified the same completed message as `duplicate`.
- What was not tested: Live WhatsApp Web crash/restart with real account traffic was not run.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Source-level issue #50563 documents the ack-before-process loss mode.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The monitor only used in-memory dedupe/read handling around `messages.upsert`; if the gateway crashed after accepting an inbound event but before `onMessage` completed, there was no authDir-backed pending record to replay.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The existing monitor checks covered dispatch and retryable redelivery, but not crash/restart persistence of accepted inbound messages.
- Contributing context (if known): WhatsApp append/notify events can be redelivered, but the old path had no durable acceptance boundary before processing.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Accept an inbound WhatsApp message, preserve it through retryable failure or restart replay, and complete it only after handler finalization.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The monitor behavior harness exercises the Baileys event-to-inbox dispatch boundary without needing a live WhatsApp session.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing cases cover dedupe, retryable redelivery, async read receipts, and close drain; no existing case fully simulates crash/restart replay.
- If no new test is added, why not: This patch keeps the shipped staged scope and relies on the existing monitor behavior suite plus manual pre-ship review; live crash/restart proof still needs a real WhatsApp session.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

WhatsApp inbound messages that reach the monitor now survive retryable handler failures or gateway restart instead of relying only on in-memory processing state.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Baileys upsert] -> [in-memory dedupe/read path] -> [handler] -> [crash can lose message]

After:
[Baileys upsert] -> [durable pending record] -> [handler] -> [complete or retry on replay]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: WhatsApp inbound message payloads are now persisted under the existing authDir. The queue is account-scoped, file-locked, prunes completed IDs, and uses the same local plugin storage boundary as other WhatsApp session files.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4.1
- Runtime/container: Node v24.14.0 local checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp plugin monitor durable inbound storage
- Relevant config (redacted): Temporary authDir only

### Steps

1. Use a temporary authDir.
2. Load `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/durable-queue.ts` with `node --import tsx`.
3. Accept one WhatsApp-shaped inbound message, record a retry, complete it, then submit the same message again.

### Expected

- The first message is accepted, remains pending after retry recording, is removed after completion, and then dedupes on redelivery.

### Actual

- The terminal capture in the Real behavior proof section shows `pendingAfterAccept: 1`, `pendingAfterRetry: 1`, `pendingAfterComplete: 0`, and `duplicateAfterComplete: "duplicate"`.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Real authDir durable queue proof with `node --import tsx --input-type=module`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`; committed-diff pre-ship review against codex-style, coderabbit-style, and greptileai-style checks.
- Edge cases checked: duplicate delivery, retryable inbound failure, read receipt timing, close drain, pending replay ordering, JSON store locking/serialization.
- What you did **not** verify: Live WhatsApp Web crash/restart replay with a real account.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Persisting inbound WhatsApp payloads increases local authDir storage sensitivity.
  - Mitigation: The store stays under the existing local WhatsApp auth/session boundary, uses account-scoped records, and prunes completed IDs.
- Risk: Read receipts are now sent after handler finalization instead of before handler dispatch.
  - Mitigation: Retryable failures avoid falsely acknowledging unread work; stale append messages still get read handling without auto-reply.
